data.title: Advance Auto Parts outlines plan to add 100+ stores and targets operating margin improvement in 2025
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Advance Auto Parts (AAP) Q1 2025

Management View

  • Shane O'Kelly, President & Chief Executive Officer, stated that "our team delivered better-than-expected first quarter results" and highlighted a rebound in demand led by the Pro business, with "eight consecutive weeks of positive comparable sales growth in the U.S." for the Pro segment. O'Kelly emphasized the completion of the store footprint optimization program, noting "approximately 75% of our store footprint is now concentrated in markets where we hold the #1 or #2 position based on store density." He announced plans to "open more than 100 new stores" over the next three years, with further acceleration possible.
  • O'Kelly detailed progress in merchandising, including the expansion of a new assortment framework to 10 additional DMAs and plans to "complete the rollout in the top 50 DMAs by the end of 2025 with 30 of the 50 markets expected to be live by August," which is faster than the previously expected timeline. He reported an estimated uplift of nearly 50 basis points in comparable sales growth within these DMAs during the first nine weeks following the rollout.
  • O'Kelly cited improvements in supply chain productivity, specifically that "product lines per hour... improved in the low-single-digit percentage range during Q1 compared to last year." He confirmed the ongoing consolidation of distribution centers, targeting a reduction to 12 large DCs by the end of 2026.
  • O'Kelly also described operational improvements in the stores, including a reduction in delivery time by approximately 10 minutes compared to last year and a focus on capturing additional wallet share in the Pro channel. He noted that a standardized store operating structure test is now live in about 10% of stores.
  • Ryan Grimsland, Executive Vice President & Chief Financial Officer, stated, "For the first quarter, net sales from continuing operations were $2.6 billion, a 7% decrease compared to last year," with the decline mainly attributed to store optimization activity. Grimsland highlighted that "adjusted operating loss from continuing operations came in at $8 million or negative 30 basis points of net sales" and "adjusted diluted loss per share from continuing operations was $0.22 compared with earnings per share of $0.33 in the prior year." He reaffirmed full year 2025 guidance and discussed the impact of tariffs and mitigation strategies.

Outlook

  • The company reaffirmed its fiscal 2025 guidance, projecting net sales in the range of $8.4 billion to $8.6 billion and comparable sales growth between 50 basis points and 150 basis points on a 52-week basis. Sequential improvement in comparable sales is expected during 2025, with stronger growth in the second half supported by initiatives to improve parts availability and service levels.
  • For Q2, management estimates "flattish comparable sales growth, including the impact of the Easter shift from Q1." Adjusted operating income margin is expected in the range of 2% to 3%, with gross margin identified as the primary driver, supported by product cost savings and supply chain leverage.
  • Adjusted diluted EPS is expected in the range of $1.50 and $2.50. Free cash flow is projected to be between negative $85 million and negative $25 million at year-end, including approximately $150 million in cash expenses associated with store and DC optimization activity.

Financial Results

  • Net sales from continuing operations were $2.6 billion for Q1 2025, a decrease of 7% year-over-year, mainly due to store closures. Comparable store sales declined 60 basis points. Liquidation sales from closed locations generated $51 million.
  • Gross profit was $1.11 billion or 42.9% of net sales, with a gross margin contraction of 50 basis points year-over-year, mainly due to "approximately 90 basis points of margin headwind associated with liquidation sales."
  • Adjusted SG&A was $1.12 billion or 43.2% of net sales. Free cash flow was negative $198 million, including $90 million in cash expenses for the store optimization project and $100 million in inventory investments to support the accelerated rollout of the assortment framework.

Q&A

  • Simeon Gutman, Morgan Stanley: Asked about the mix of DIY versus DIFM within the comp guidance and any changes in expectations based on Q1 trends. Ryan Grimsland responded, "We haven't seen any change in trend on DIY and our expectations, but what we have stated is that we expect DIFM to be the driver of our performance. The DIY will still be somewhat pressured."
  • Gutman also inquired about gross margin and SG&A outlook, noting upside drivers. Grimsland explained, "our driver of operating income, will mainly be driven by gross profit growth, and especially in the back half of the year," with SG&A expected to be "down year-over-year, especially with the store closures."
  • Seth Sigman, Barclays: Asked about the impact of store closures on comps and sales transfer. Grimsland clarified that "the comp difference between those closing stores and the remaining stores wasn't material." He added that some transfer sales were achieved, mainly in the Pro area.
  • Sigman followed up on tariff impacts and pricing. Grimsland outlined mitigation strategies, emphasizing pushback on cost increases, alternative supply sources, and passing through remaining costs to price.
  • Chris Horvers, JPMorgan: Inquired about inflation, tariff pricing, and LIFO impacts. Grimsland stated, "the inflation impact in Q1 was immaterial," and LIFO favorability was approximately $4 million in the quarter.
  • Michael Lasser, UBS: Questioned ability to achieve 2027 margin goals if DIY remains flat. O'Kelly asserted the importance of DIY initiatives and vendor partnerships, while Grimsland noted that low-single-digit comp growth, led by DIFM, supports the 7% margin target.
  • Zach Fadem, Wells Fargo: Asked about non-GAAP adjustments and vendor financing. Grimsland indicated $90 million of the $150 million cash expenses for the year are done, and current supply chain finance usage stands at about $3 billion.
  • Scot Ciccarelli, Truist: Asked about gross margin drivers. Grimsland responded that "the bulk of our gross margin is going to be coming from COGS improvement" and that procurement cost reductions will continue as contracts are executed.
  • Steven Zaccone, Citigroup: Asked about improvement in Pro business and Q2 comp composition. O'Kelly emphasized internal execution, including improved delivery times and availability, as key drivers.
  • Steven Forbes, Guggenheim: Inquired about the store operating model and market share implications of store density. O'Kelly explained the focus on asset allocation and the advantages of being #1 or #2 in store density for capturing market share.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts maintained a neutral to slightly positive tone, recognizing progress but pressing for clarity on guidance sensitivity, tariff impacts, and gross margin sustainability. They focused on structural challenges in DIY and the durability of procurement savings.
  • Management expressed cautious optimism, as reflected in O'Kelly's statements about "positive momentum in Pro," and a focus on "controlling the aspects of our business within our control while navigating a volatile macro environment." The tone was constructive but included reminders of volatility and the need for ongoing mitigation efforts, particularly regarding tariffs.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone shifted from stabilization and foundational changes to emphasizing operational execution, accelerated rollout of strategic initiatives, and early turnaround proof points. Analyst sentiment remained measured, with persistent scrutiny on guidance credibility and margin improvement.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for net sales, comparable sales, operating margin, and EPS remained consistent with previous quarter levels, but management increased the pace of strategic rollout, notably with the assortment framework and new store expansion.
  • Strategic focus shifted from foundational transformation activities (e.g., store closures, divestitures) to execution of operational initiatives and capturing market share in core markets.
  • Analysts continued to focus on the trajectory of margin improvement, sustainability of procurement cost reductions, and the dynamics of the Pro vs. DIY mix.
  • Key metric changes included a sequential improvement in store availability KPI and progress toward positive operating margins starting in Q2. The company moved from discussing anticipated improvement to reporting initial positive outcomes in Pro comps and operational metrics.
  • Management's confidence level increased, supported by early results from the accelerated rollout of strategic initiatives and improved performance in the Pro segment.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management called out the volatility and uncertainty in the tariff environment as a key risk, with O'Kelly noting "tariff creates variability by product, country of origin, tariff magnitude... the stackability of a tariff." Grimsland detailed mitigation strategies, including alternative sourcing and selective price increases.
  • The DIY channel remains pressured, with persistent consumer caution flagged as a challenge. Management is proactively addressing this with training, marketing, and improved in-store experience but acknowledged that the environment "remains challenged due to the potential for higher broad-based consumer goods inflation impacting household budgets."
  • Analysts raised concerns about the durability of procurement cost reductions, the effect of store closures on comp and market share, and the ability to manage through inflationary and tariff-driven cost headwinds.

Final Takeaway

Advance Auto Parts leadership highlighted early signs of operational turnaround, with sequential improvements in Pro comps, supply chain productivity, and margin trajectory. Management reaffirmed full-year guidance and outlined accelerated rollout of key strategic initiatives, including the opening of over 100 new stores in core markets and expedited deployment of the assortment framework. While the DIY environment remains pressured and tariff risks persist, the company is leaning into mitigation strategies and operational execution to drive long-term profitable growth and strengthen its competitive position.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Gilead to buy certain Hookipa Pharma assets for up to $10M
data.description:
Antimicrobial drug developer HOOKIPA Pharma (NASDAQ:HOOK) announced Thursday that Gilead Sciences (NASDAQ:GILD) has agreed to acquire some of its assets for a total consideration of up to $10M.

In a regulatory filing, the New York-based biotech said that Gilead (NASDAQ:GILD) is set to buy its assets primarily related to its antiviral programs HB-400 and HB-500 under an asset purchase agreement signed on Wednesday.

HOOKIPA (NASDAQ:HOOK) was advancing the programs targeted at Hepatitis B and Human Immunodeficiency Virus, respectively, as part of a collaboration with Gilead (NASDAQ:GILD).

In return, Gilead (GILD) has agreed to pay $3M to the company at the closing of the deal. HOOK is also eligible to receive up to $7M in three stages under a three-phase asset transfer plan, with $3M due after the completion of the first phase and $2M each following the subsequent phases.  

The deal’s completion is subject to several closing conditions, including the approval of HOOKIPA’s (NASDAQ:HOOK) stockholders.

data.title: Nvidia, OpenAI among big tech bringing Stargate AI project to UAE
data.description:
Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA), Oracle (NYSE:ORCL), OpenAI, Cisco Systems (NASDAQ:CSCO) , SoftBank Group (OTCPK:SFTBY) (OTCPK:SFTBF) and G42 are collaborating to build Stargate UAE.

In January, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, SoftBank's CEO Masayoshi Son and Oracle's Co-Founder and Chairman Larry Ellison joined U.S. President Donald Trump at the White House to officially announce a $500B four-year initiative called Stargate to build out AI infrastructure.

United Arab Emirates, or UAE, based G42 said on Thursday that Stargate UAE is a next-generation AI infrastructure cluster that will run in the newly established 5-gigawatt UAE-U.S. AI Campus in Abu Dhabi.

OpenAI and G42, both AI companies, are backed by Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT).

Stargate UAE, a 1-gigawatt compute cluster, will be built by G42 and operated by OpenAI and Oracle. 

The collaboration will also include Cisco, which will offer its zero-trust security and AI-ready connectivity, and SoftBank Group, as well as Nvidia, which will supply its latest Grace Blackwell GB300 systems.

The first 200-megawatt AI cluster is expected to go live in 2026.

The facility will provide best-in-class infrastructure, nation-scale compute, and low-latency inferencing to deliver AI that will meet the demands of an increasingly intelligent world, according to G42.

 

 

The UAE-U.S. AI Campus, which will house Stargate UAE was announced last week in Abu Dhabi in the presence of UAE President Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan and U.S. President Donald  Trump, who was a visit to the Middle East a few days ago.

Last week, it was reported that OpenAI could become a “key anchor” of the new 5 gigawatt data center project unveiled in the UAE.

The new endeavor builds on a new framework by the U.S. and UAE governments called the “U.S.-UAE AI Acceleration Partnership" to bolster cooperation and collaboration on AI and advanced technologies, developing safe, secure, and responsible AI that delivers long-term benefits for humanity, the company added.

The full UAE–U.S. AI infrastructure campus will span 10 square miles and is the largest such deployment outside the U.S., G42 noted.

It will provide 5 gigawatts of AI data center capacity and regional compute resources.

"By establishing the world’s first Stargate outside of the U.S. in the UAE, we’re transforming a bold vision into reality. This is the first major milestone in our OpenAI for Countries initiative—our effort to work with allies and partners to build AI infrastructure around the world," said OpenAI's Co-Founder and CEO Sam Altman.

Earlier this month, it was reported that OpenAI planned to expand the Stargate project by investing more overseas to promote the development of “democratic artificial intelligence.” 

The facility will be powered by nuclear, solar, and natural gas to minimize carbon emissions and it will also house a science park for innovation, talent development, and sustainable computing infrastructure.

"This first-in-the-world platform will enable every UAE government agency and commercial institution to connect their data to the world’s most advanced AI models," said Larry Ellison, CTO and Chairman of Oracle.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, and Dow look for direction after U.S. House passes Trump's major tax bill
data.description:
Wall Street major averages look for direction on Thursday, while a bond sell-off in longer-term maturities picked up pace after the U.S. House narrowly passed President Trump's "One Big Beautiful" tax bill.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was near even, the NasdaqComposite (COMP:IND) was +0.5%, while the Dow (DJI) was -0.2%.

The 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) ticked higher, last up 2 basis points to 5.10%. Other maturities were mixed following a bruising bond sell-off in the previous session. The 10-year yield (US10Y) was lower by 1 basis point to 4.58%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) was down 2 basis points to 3.99%.

The U.S. House narrowly passed the multitrillion-dollar sweeping package with a vote of 215-214, after days of marathon meetings and intense negotiations. The "One Big Beautiful Bill" will now head to the Senate, where it is expected to meet sharp resistance.   

Stocks sold off in the previous session and bonds sold off sharply, pushing yields higher, with a weak Treasury auction spooking traders already worried about the debt implications of the tax bill.

"The most troubling part of the market reaction (to the auction) is that the dollar (DXY) is weakening at the same time," Deutsche Bank's George Saravelos said. "To us this is a clear signal of a foreign buyer's strike on US assets and the associated US fiscal risks we have been warning for some time."

"At the core of the problem is that foreign investors are simply no longer willing to finance US twin deficits at current level of prices."

"It is hard for US equities to stay resilient in this environment,"  Saravelos added. "The 2023-24 period saw a combined rise in US yields and equities as the market was revising US growth expectations higher. This was entirely reasonable. Today is very different."

"It is all a building fiscal risk premium in to US assets. It is hard to make the case that such a (negative) driver of the rising cost of capital is positive for risk assets."

"The details of the budget are constantly changing, so their effects are hard to judge, but the broad impact is to push the US further along a rising debt path," UBS' Paul Donovan said. "Bond investors are less than happy."

"Economically quite a lot of the budget is extending existing tax cuts, which does not add to growth. Removing taxes on tips might push inflation higher than reported. If employers reduce pay (included in consumer price data via prices) and encourage tipping (not included in consumer price data), the cost of living will rise more than reported inflation."

On the economic calendar, the S&P Global U.S. Composite PMI rose to 52.1 in May's flash estimate, exceeding the 50.3 consensus

Initial jobless claims for the week ended May 17 dropped by 2K to 227K versus the 230K consensus level.

U.S. existing home sales also dipped 0.5% M/M to 4.000M in April, compared with the 4.130M forecasted figure.

"The greater uncertainty and additional uptick in longterm interest rates accompanying the tariff shock so far will add to the headwinds facing housing at the margin," Pantheon Macro's Samuel Tombs said. "But the fundamental predicament for the housing market has been little changed since early 2023."

"Mortgage rates of around 7% are high enough to discourage many new buyers, while the big gap between market rates and the average rates on existing mortgages is encouraging many existing homeowners to stay put, also reducing the number of homes coming on the market and keeping prices relatively high."

data.title: IonQ jumps as CEO tells Barron's it wants to become 'Nvidia of quantum computing'
data.description:
• IonQ (NYSE:IONQ) shares jumped more than 14% on Thursday after CEO Niccolo de Masi told Barron's it wants to become the Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) of quantum computing.
  • “We’re in the business of quantum just like Nvidia and Broadcom are in the business of classical GPUs,” de Masi told the news outlet. “I believe IonQ will be the Nvidia player. There will be other people that copy us and follow us; they have always copied and followed us.”
  • “We feel good about our competitive positioning because, at the end of the day, we are trying to drive ecosystem,” de Masi added.
  • Shares of other quantum computing stocks also rose on Thursday, including Quantum Computing (QUBT), D-Wave Quantum (QBTS) and Arqit Quantum (ARQQ). IonQ shares are down 11% year-to-date, but have gained 344% over the past 12 months.
  • Investor interest in the sector surged last year after Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) unveiled its quantum computing chip, code-named Willow. Since then, Microsoft (MSFT) and Amazon (AMZN) have introduced quantum computing chips of their own.

data.title: Titan Machinery's shares surge as Q1 revenue jumps in Europe
data.description:
Titan Machinery (NASDAQ:TITN) surged as much as 17% Thursday morning before trimming its gain. The farm and construction equipment dealer posted fiscal first-quarter results that showed progress on inventory management and overseas growth.

The company swung to a net loss of $13.2 million, or $0.58 a share, for the quarter ended April 30, from net income of $9.4 million, or $0.41 a share, in the same period last year.

Revenue came in at $594.3 million, a 5.5% decline from $628.7 million a year ago.

Titan’s (NASDAQ:TITN) first-quarter earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization plummeted to $2.6 million from $30.9 million a year earlier.

“While headwinds persist across the agricultural sector, our team remains focused on continuing to execute upon our initiative to optimize inventory,” Bryan Knutson, chief executive of Titan (TITN), said in a statement.

Revenue driven by pre-sold equipment

Knutson cautioned that the better-than-expected revenue was primarily the result of timing on deliveries of pre-sold equipment, rather than any meaningful uptick in market demand. The agriculture sector, Titan’s (TITN) largest revenue source, remains under pressure due to weak commodity prices and tight farm margins.

The Agriculture segment posted $384.4 million in revenue, down 14% year over year, with a $12.8 million pre-tax loss. Titan (TITN) maintained its guidance for a full-year revenue decline of 20% to 25% in this core business.

Meanwhile, Construction segment revenue was flat at $72.1 million, with a small pre-tax loss of $4.2 million, while Australia saw relatively stable results despite foreign exchange headwinds.

European operations shine

The standout performer was Titan’s (TITMN) Europe segment, where revenue jumped nearly 44% year over year (adjusted for currency) to $93.9 million. That growth, driven largely by strong demand in Romania supported by EU stimulus funding, helped generate $4.7 million in pre-tax income, more than triple the year-ago result.

As a result, the company raised its full-year guidance for European revenue growth to 23% to 28%, up from a previous estimate of flat to 5%.

In contrast, the Australia segment recorded $44 million in revenue, nearly unchanged from last year, but guidance was lowered to a 20% to 25% annual decline amid local headwinds.

Inventory strategy gains ground

Titan’s (TITN) gross margin fell to 15.3% from 19.4% a year earlier, primarily because of aggressive pricing actions aimed at moving inventory in a sluggish retail environment, the company said. Gross profit was $90.9 million, down from $121.8 million.

Despite tighter margins, the company saw a sequential 15% decline in floorplan and other interest expenses, reflecting progress in inventory control, a central pillar of Titan’s (TITN) current strategy. Inventory levels remained flat at $1.1 billion quarter-over-quarter, while cash from operations improved to $6.2 million from a $32.4 million outflow a year ago.

Outlook unchanged

Titan (TITN) reaffirmed its full-year guidance of an adjusted loss between $1.25 and $2.00 a share. While management acknowledged continued uncertainty in the farm economy, including undefined government support programs, it expressed confidence in its operational focus and long-term positioning.

“Absent unique variables, the broader agriculture sector remains challenged in the near-term,” Knutson said. “The progression of our inventory reduction efforts remains core to our operating strategy to stabilize equipment margins and restore the business's earnings power.”

data.title: Ralph Lauren raises dividend by 10.6% to $0.9125
data.description:
• Ralph Lauren (NYSE:RL) declares $0.9125/share quarterly dividend, 10.6% increase from prior dividend of $0.8250.
  • Forward yield 1.31%
  • Payable July 11; for shareholders of record June 27; ex-div June 27.
  • See RL Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Unum to hike dividend by ~10%
data.description:
Unum Group (NYSE:UNM) announced on Thursday an authorization by the board to increase its quarterly dividend by ~10%, effective the third quarter of 2025.

The workplace benefits and services company last paid a $0.42/share quarterly dividend, which represents a payout of $1.68 on an annual basis.

The new rate of $0.46 per share brings the payout to $1.84 per share on an annual basis.

data.title: freenet AG reports Q1 results
data.description:
• freenet AG press release (OTCPK:FRTAF): Q1 Revenues increased moderately by 1.7% to EUR 604.4 million (prior year: EUR 594.5 million)
  • Adjusted EBITDA of EUR 126.1 million at the prior year's level (EUR 127.1 million)
  • Free cash flow increased by 1.7% to EUR 75.8 million (prior year: EUR 74.5 million)
  • Guidance for the full year 2025 confirmed.
  • The solid business development confirms the guidance for the Group to achieve an adjusted EBITDA of EUR 520 million to EUR 540 million by the end of the year (prior year: EUR 503.1 million) and a free cash flow of EUR 300 million to EUR 320 million (prior year: EUR 292.3 million).
